subject: [PATCH 3/4] arm64: wire SDEI NMI into the hardlockup watchdog

2026-06-11.patch_summary: This patch adds support for the SDEI (System Description Event Interface) NMI to the hardlockup watchdog on arm64 architectures. It introduces a new driver that uses SDEI to deliver cross-CPU NMIs, which can be used by various kernel features such as arch_trigger_cpumask_backtrace and the hardlockup watchdog backend. The patch also selects HAVE_HARDLOCKUP_DETECTOR_ARCH in Kconfig to enable this feature. If the firmware provides SDEI, it is used; otherwise, a perf-NMI counter fallback is employed.

It introduces a new driver that uses SDEI to deliver cross-CPU NMIs, which can be used by various kernel features such as arch_trigger_cpumask_backtrace and the hardlockup watchdog backend. The patch also selects HAVE_HARDLOCKUP_DETECTOR_ARCH in Kconfig to enable this feature. If the firmware provides SDEI, it is used; otherwise, a perf-NMI counter fallback is employed.
